title: Evangelion and the Right Hand
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and the Right Hand             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Jan 12, 2007 1:55 am In Evangelion, there are many instances where the Right hand is used a few notable ones are: 1. Kaworu is "killed" with Evangelion Unit 01's right hand 2. When Shinji first meets Rei, her right hand is bandaged 3. When Shinji..."Relieves" himself by Asuka's bedside on "End of Evangelion", he uses his right hand. 4. The Adam embryo is embedded in Gendo's right hand. 5. During the last scene in "End of Evangelion", Asuka's right hand is bandaged just like Rei's. What exactly is the meaning behind this use of the right hand? Is all this just a coincidence, or is there a hidden meaning behind it?

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Fri Jan 12, 2007 1:56 am i seriously doubt it... Shinji jerking off...right hand = hand of "swords"...hehe left handed people are better in bed anyway

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 12, 2007 2:03 am I love fanwanking as much as the next person but there is a point where you're just overanalyzing things. Most people are right-handed plain and simple.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Jan 12, 2007 3:42 am Rei: What is your hand for? The hand is used to take action. To affect or manipulate the world around you. In Evangelion hands form a recurring visual motif. Shinji is constantly clenching and unclenching his fist, representing his inability or unwillingness to make a decision or him making a decision and wanting to unmake it or negate it with a later action. What hand action do you think Shinji might later have regretted? ^ Hands are also used for fanwankery. (Â½ Serious)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 12, 2007 4:37 am Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: In Evangelion hands form a recurring visual motif.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Fri Jan 12, 2007 12:25 pm Reichu wrote: Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: In Evangelion hands form a recurring visual motif.

[]: Re: Evangelion and the Right Hand             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jan 12, 2007 12:45 pm KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: What exactly is the meaning behind this use of the right hand? Is all this just a coincidence, or is there a hidden meaning behind it?

[]: Re: Evangelion and the Right Hand             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 12, 2007 1:27 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Shinji's amgiguous clenching and unclenching of his hand which is shown throughout the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 12, 2007 1:52 pm Szmitten wrote: Opening a hatch with a free hand while you're falling to pieces and carrying a child in the other =/= being left-handed.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Jan 12, 2007 2:11 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Which led me to not notice *that* scene because I'd just thought "He's staring at his f---ing hand again, the tosser !" and didn't bother to look at the extra detail.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 12, 2007 3:15 pm Shinji is constantly clenching and unclenching his fist, representing his inability or unwillingness to make a decision or him making a decision and wanting to unmake it or negate it with a later action.

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Fri Jan 12, 2007 3:41 pm Perhaps the right hand is like sunglasses in the Matrix. Good guys had round sunglass, while bad guys had square. And when Neo punches Smith, he breaks his singlasses.

[]:             Quote Post by Rawinder » Fri Jan 12, 2007 8:20 pm Reichu wrote: Szmitten wrote: Opening a hatch with a free hand while you're falling to pieces and carrying a child in the other =/= being left-handed.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Fri Jan 12, 2007 8:37 pm Doesn't matter. I fap with my left! ...<_<

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Jan 12, 2007 8:40 pm He had both hands when he was walking towards the camara Rawinder. His gloves are burnt for some reason though. When he opens the hatch with his left hand isn't he holding his daughter with his right hand/arm? (I wouldn't use my weaker hand to hold something as important as that. ) When he does that his gloves are melting ouch. When Shinji and Misato were a mile or so from an N2 explosion they only got knocked backwards.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jan 12, 2007 9:44 pm @Raw: What KnT said. Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: His gloves are burnt for some reason though.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sun Jan 14, 2007 9:29 pm something else i noticed...EVERYONE in the show has hands...Rei used her HANDS to eat teh ramen...what coudl it mean??? ...honestly though ill further your fanwaking... noticed which hand Shinji squeezes Rei gazonkos...(same with Gendo)...

[]:             Quote Post by brainDamage » Sun Jan 14, 2007 9:45 pm It was long believed in Christian (and other societies) that the right hand is the hand that does good, or is "right." Left is associated with devil, wiping your ass, underhanded 'nare do wells and that sort of thing. A simple wikipedia look up would provide enough ammunition for quite a discussion on the symbolism of the right hand in Evangelion. The entry is quite thorough. [url]http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Left-handed[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jan 14, 2007 9:54 pm master0rolando wrote: something else i noticed...EVERYONE in the show has hands...

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Jan 14, 2007 10:10 pm I profoundly resent the implication that I am inherently evil. ... ... ... Okay, so I am evil, but being left-handed has nothing to do with it!
